title: carex tomentosa subsp. nordmannii
intro: Carex tomentosa subsp. nordmannii, commonly known as the woolly sedge, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Carex tomentosa subsp. nordmannii include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
